title: Triage analysis/candidate_tickets.jsonl: 7 untriaged auto-mined candidates
current_state: scripts/passive/ticket_candidate_miner.py has mined 7 candidate tickets into analysis/candidate_tickets.jsonl and its passive check FAILs because they sit unreviewed. Before the T-249..T-269 batch, tickets/open/ was empty, so the sprint runner had zero input - untriaged candidates are exactly the pipeline gap that left sprint.py unproven (see T-256).
target_state: Each of the 7 candidates is either promoted to a real tickets/open/T-NNN-*.json (schema per tickets/closed/T-247-*.json, with root_cause_confidence set honestly: 'verified' only if the diagnosis was reproduced) or rejected with a one-line reason recorded in the report/row. ticket_candidate_miner passive returns PASS. Any small verified promotion in a SAFE component (scripts/, testing/, docs/, vault/) is flagged as a T-256 sprint candidate.
migration_plan: 2. For each candidate: reproduce or verify the claim in code; decide promote vs reject.
3. Promote: write tickets/open/T-NNN-slug.json continuing the ID sequence after T-269; set root_cause_confidence honestly.
4. Reject: record a one-line reason (append to the candidate row or the miner report - follow whatever convention the miner already uses).
6. python scripts/refresh_pi.py so PI.md section 8 lists any promotions.
risk_notes: Do not bulk-promote - the miner produces hypotheses, and hypothesis-confidence tickets correctly cannot auto-run in sprint (T-154). The value here is the honest verified/rejected sort, not the count.
